§6Schrabidic Acid§f

§aAn incredibly dangerous corrosive liquid — essential for schrabidium processing and ferric schrabidate!§f

§bSchrabidic Acid§f is a strongly corrosive, radioactive liquid with NFPA 704 health and reactivity ratings of 4. Despite its high viscosity, it flows faster than water but spreads like lava. It heavily irradiates the player on contact.

§6Production§f (Chemical Factory — 5s, 5.0 kHE/t):
§b• 1× Schrabidium Trisulfide§f (8,000 mB)
§b• 1× Ionized Particles§f
§b• 6,000 mB Hydrogen Peroxide§f
§b→§f §e16,000 mB§f Schrabidic Acid

§6Key Uses§f:
§b• Ferric Schrabidate§f — 250 mB acid + 1 Iron Powder
§b• PUREX Schrabidium Extraction§f — extract schrabidium from depleted ZIRNOX/PWR/MEN fuel (500 mB, yields 0.75 ingots)
§b• Schrabidium Trisulfide§f — precursor chemical

§eTip§f: Each PUREX schrabidium extraction requires 500 mB acid and yields 0.75 ingots. Stockpile hydrogen peroxide and set up a reliable ionized particle source from your fusion reactor before large-scale production!
